title: Isachne rhabdina
taxonomy: Isachne rhabdina is classified within the family Poaceae and is a type of grass. It is further classified in the genus Isachne. Isachne rhabdina is native to Africa and belongs to the tribe Isachneae. The classification of Isachne rhabdina is also supported by the APG IV system, which categorizes it under the clade commelinids. Isachne rhabdina is a member of the order Poales.

intro: Isachne rhabdina, also known as Wild Rice Grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits in reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting digestion. The bioactive constituents of Isachne rhabdina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Isachne rhabdina is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
